en.extract:
MacGillivray's prion (Pachyptila macgillivrayi)  is a species of small petrel (a prion) in the Southern Ocean. It is found on Roche Quille, off Saint Paul Island and on Gough Island in the Tristan da Cunha group, south-central Atlantic Ocean. It was formerly present on Amsterdam Island in the central South Indian Ocean. The population on Saint Paul Island has been increasing since the 1990s eradication of introduced rats and rabbits, but is still likely smaller than the original size. 
MacGillivray's prion was formerly considered to be conspecific with Salvin's prion but is now considered to be a separate species based on molecular phylogenetic analysis and a comparison of the bill morphologies that was published in 2022.
MacGillivray's prions moult for longer than other prion species.
Their diet and foraging habits are generally unknown, but they are hypothesized to feed on small zooplankton.

'''MacGillivray's prion''' ('''''Pachyptila macgillivrayi''''')  is a [[species]] of small [[petrel]] (a [[prion (bird)|prion]]) in the [[Southern Ocean]]. It is found on Roche Quille, off [[Île Saint-Paul|Saint Paul Island]] and on [[Gough Island]] in the [[Tristan da Cunha]] group, south-central Atlantic Ocean. It was formerly present on [[Amsterdam Island]] in the central South Indian Ocean. The population on Saint Paul Island has been increasing since the 1990s eradication of introduced rats and rabbits, but is still likely smaller than the original size.<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal |last1=Delord |first1=K |last2=Cherel |first2=Y |last3=Roy |first3=A |last4=Bustamante |first4=P |last5=Swadling |first5=KM |last6=Weimerskirch |first6=H |last7=Bost |first7=CA |last8=Barbraud |first8=C |date=2022-09-22 |title=At-sea behavioural ecology of the endangered MacGillivray's prion from Saint Paul Island: combining tracking and stable isotopes |journal=Marine Ecology Progress Series |volume=697 |pages=149–165 |doi=10.3354/meps14136 |s2cid=251166177 |issn=0171-8630}}</ref> 

MacGillivray's prion was formerly considered to be [[conspecific]] with [[Salvin's prion]] but is now considered to be a separate species based on [[molecular phylogenetic]] analysis and a comparison of the bill morphologies that was published in 2022.<ref>{{Cite journal | last1=Masello | first1=J.F. | last2=Ryan | first2=P.G. | last3=Shepherd | first3=L.D. | last4=Quillfeldt | first4=P. | last5=Cherel | first5=Y. | last6=Tennyson | first6=A.J.D. | last7=Alderman | first7=R. | last8=Calderón | first8=L. | last9=Cole | first9=T.L. | last10=Cuthbert | first10=R.J. | last11=Dilley | first11=B.J. | last12=Massaro | first12=M. | last13=Miskelly | first13=C.M. | last14=Navarro | first14=J. | last15=Phillips | first15=R.A. | last16=Weimerskirch | first16=H. | last17=Moodley | first17=Y. | date=2022 | title=Independent evolution of intermediate bill widths in a seabird clade | journal=Molecular Genetics and Genomics | volume=297 | issue=1 | pages=183–198 | doi=10.1007/s00438-021-01845-3 | pmid=34921614 | pmc=8803701 | doi-access=free }}</ref><ref name=ioc>{{cite web| editor1-last=Gill | editor1-first=Frank | editor1-link=Frank Gill (ornithologist) | editor2-last=Donsker | editor2-first=David | editor3-last=Rasmussen | editor3-first=Pamela | editor3-link=Pamela Rasmussen | date=July 2022 | title=Petrels, albatrosses | work=IOC World Bird List Version 12.2 | url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/bow/petrels/ | publisher=International Ornithologists' Union | access-date=26 August 2022 }}</ref>

MacGillivray's prions moult for longer than other prion species.<ref name=":0" />

Their diet and foraging habits are generally unknown, but they are hypothesized to feed on small zooplankton.<ref name=":0" />
